title: V-Model artifact envelope
description: Universal front-matter shape common to every V-Model artifact: the six core standalone types (Product Brief, Requirements, Architecture, ADR, Detailed Design, TestSpec) plus bundle-detail types that compose into a helicopter parent (currently architecture-interface-detail; further bundle subtypes may be added). Per-artifact schemas extend this envelope via allOf, add type-specific fields (scope, parent_scope, derived_from, governing_adrs, scope_tags, affected_scopes, belongs_to, kind, subject, …), and tighten constraints on id. Validating front-matter against the envelope alone answers 'is this a recognisable V-Model artifact?'; validating against the per-artifact schema answers 'is it a well-formed instance of its type?'.

id.description: Stable artifact identifier. Per-artifact schemas tighten to a type-specific pattern — PB for Product Brief, REQ-{scope}-{seq} for Requirements, ARCH-{scope} for Architecture, DD-{scope}-{name} for Detailed Design, TS-{scope} for TestSpec, ADR-{seq}-{slug} for ADR. IDs are stable across file renames; traceability links reference IDs, not paths.

title.description: Human-readable one-line title. Appears as the document header.

artifact_type.description: Type discriminator. Redundant with filename convention (product_brief.md, requirements.md, …) but required so an artifact identifies itself without filesystem context.

status.description: Lifecycle state. Required and non-empty at envelope level; the per-artifact schema constrains the specific vocabulary. Most artifacts use the universal lifecycle (draft | active | superseded) via common-defs#/$defs/lifecycle_status; ADR overrides to Nygard's proposed | accepted | superseded. The envelope intentionally does not mandate an enum so per-artifact schemas can choose their vocabulary without fighting schema intersection rules.

date.description: Date the artifact was last materially modified.

version.description: Monotonic integer version of this artifact. Bumped on material revisions.

recovery_status.description: Retrofit provenance. Absent on greenfield artifacts. Scalar form applies to the whole artifact; object form targets named fields. The no-fabrication rule is enforced per-artifact, not here.

supersedes.description: Artifact ID that this artifact replaces. Used when a differently-named artifact takes over from a predecessor (common for ADRs; rare elsewhere). Routine revisions bump the version field instead of issuing a superseding artifact.

superseded_by.description: Artifact ID that replaces this artifact. Set when status transitions to superseded.

belongs_to.description: Bundle back-link. Set on detail-file artifacts (e.g. architecture-interface-detail) to point at the helicopter artifact whose bundle they belong to. Tooling treats helicopter + bundle detail files as one logical artifact. Absent on standalone artifacts (Rule 8 in docs/authoring-discipline.md).

kind.description: Bundle-detail subtype discriminator. Required on detail-file artifacts; mirrors the artifact_type field for the bundle case. Example: 'architecture-interface-detail'. Free-string at envelope level so future bundle subtypes can be added without envelope edits; per-artifact schemas pin the const value.

subject.description: Bundle-detail subject. Required on detail-file artifacts; identifies which element of the helicopter this detail file documents. Example: for architecture-interface-detail, the interface name (IValidate). Free-string here; per-artifact schemas tighten the pattern when applicable.
